rationale: Both correctly trace the guard to PR #4834 (matches yenuo26's comment) but then both confidently fabricate a specific closing fix (PR #4912, level=1 change) and declare the issue resolved/closed — while the actual thread shows Flink-ddd still asking to trigger a full sleep-mode CI run, implying the issue was still under active investigation, not resolved. That's a confident-wrong pattern for both. X is slightly better because it appends an explicit caveat that merge-state claims were 'not verified via gh this run — treat as unconfirmed,' whereas Y asserts unverified specifics (an exact merge date, extra test names/line numbers, 'already in main') with no hedging at all, compounding the hallucination risk. Y is somewhat more complete, adding practical runtime guidance for users choosing between sleep levels, which slightly helps the reporter's broader situation.
